page: Microsoft Azure F80amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famdsv7 family. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 640.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This server includes 9449 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. It does not include any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F80amds_v7 offers high resource density, combining a large memory footprint with substantial local storage capacity on a single virtual machine. The primary tradeoff is the lack of hardware-accelerated graphics processing units, which limits its utility for deep learning or rendering tasks.
The hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database hosting, enterprise resource planning systems, and in-memory data analytics.
description: Microsoft Azure F80amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors, paired with 640.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities include 9449 GB of local disk capacity alongside premium storage compatibility. The server does not contain any graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This configuration provides high resource density for memory-intensive applications and large-scale data processing. The F80amds_v7 is structured for workloads such as in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and large-scale analytical processing where substantial memory and local storage are required.
